content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

June 20, 1990

Carolyn Wallace, Executive Director  
South Orange County Leadership Conference  
P.O. Box 3178  
Mission Viejo, CA 92690

Re: Your Request for Confirmation of Telephone Advice  
Our File No. A-90-392

Dear Ms. Wallace:

We have received your letter dated May 29, 1990 seeking written confirmation of telephone advice provided to you by this agency on May 16, 1990 regarding the responsibilities of your organization, the South Orange County Leadership Conference ("SOCLC"), under the mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act ("the Act").1 Your letter generally reflects the advice given to you, but clarification is necessary.

You were advised simply that the Act’s prohibition on mass mailings sent at public expense (Section 89001; Regulation 18901, copy enclosed) did not appear applicable to the SOCLC, a nonprofit association. This advice was premised on the understanding that the evaluative criteria for determining whether an entity was, in fact, a "local governmental agency" (In re Siegel (1977) 3 FPPC Ops. 62, copy enclosed), were not met.2 If SOCLC is not a local governmental agency for purposes of the Act, it is not subject to Section 89001’s prohibition on sending mass mailings at public expense.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 In determining whether an entity was a local government agency, In re Siegel considered (1) whether the impetus for the formation of the entity originated with a government agency; (2) whether the entity was substantially funded by, or its primary source of funds was, a government agency; (3) whether one of the principal purposes for which the entity was formed was to provide services or undertake obligations which public agencies are legally authorized to perform and which, in fact, they traditionally have performed; and (4) whether the entity was treated as a public entity by other statutory provisions. You indicated on May 16, 1990 that none of these criteria were applicable to SOCLC.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you requested. If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JSR:plh
Enclosures
